project.name: happymom-photography
project.description: Production-ready booking platform for a newborn & maternity photography studio — Laravel 13 Starter Kit (Inertia + React) · Tailwind CSS · MySQL. Clients browse session categories and packages, submit booking inquiries with preferred dates, and track their booking through to gallery delivery. Tickets follow a vertical-slice philosophy: each ticket cuts through database, backend, and frontend so the learner sees a working result after every task.
starter_kit_provides: Full authentication: register, login, logout, forgot/reset password
Profile page (edit name, email, delete account)

milestones.title: M0 - Foundation — Clean Slate & Project Identity
milestones.description: Strip the starter kit boilerplate down to a clean canvas, wire up MySQL, brand the app with the studio's warm aesthetic, and set up the three layout shells (public site, client account, admin panel). After this milestone the app carries HappyMom Photography's identity, has a working database, and all three section skeletons are ready to receive features.

milestones.title: M1 - User Roles & Admin Gate
milestones.description: Extend the starter kit's user system with roles (client/admin), build the admin middleware, seed an admin account, and verify the whole flow end-to-end: register as a client → can't access /admin, log in as admin → lands on the admin dashboard.

milestones.title: M2 - Session Categories (Full Vertical Slice)
milestones.description: First real feature built through every layer. The learner creates the session_categories table, model, admin CRUD pages, and public category cards — ending with session types visible on the studio site. Complete Lego piece.

milestones.title: M3 - Packages (Full Vertical Slice)
milestones.description: The core sellable unit. Session packages (e.g. Mini Newborn, Premium Maternity) with pricing, inclusions, and duration. Each ticket is a self-contained slice: create a package → see it in the admin list → see it on the public pricing page → view its detail page with a booking CTA.

milestones.title: M4 - Portfolio Gallery (Full Vertical Slice)
milestones.description: The studio's visual showcase. Admin uploads portfolio images tagged by session category. Public visitors see a filterable gallery that demonstrates the photographer's work and builds trust before booking.

milestones.title: M5 - Booking Flow (Full Vertical Slice)
milestones.description: The primary transaction: a client selects a package, chooses preferred session dates, optionally adds extras, and submits a booking inquiry. After this milestone a client can browse → choose a package → submit a booking → see a confirmation page, and the admin sees the new inquiry in the dashboard.

milestones.title: M6 - Client Account Dashboard
milestones.description: The authenticated client panel: booking list, booking detail with status timeline, and profile management. Each ticket is a standalone page the learner can visit and test immediately after placing a test booking.

milestones.title: M7 - Admin: Bookings & Customers
milestones.description: Admin-side management of booking inquiries (view, confirm date, update status through the full session lifecycle) and clients (list, detail, activate/deactivate). Each ticket produces a working admin page.

milestones.title: M8 - Albums, Add-ons & Pricing Page
milestones.description: Upsell products: physical and digital albums, and individual add-ons (rush editing, extra prints, canvas). Admin manages the catalog; clients can select add-ons when booking. Closes with a comprehensive public pricing page.

milestones.title: M9 - Static Pages & Core Content
milestones.description: Public-facing content pages: Homepage (hero, featured packages, gallery preview, testimonials), About the Studio, Contact form, Services/FAQ. Each ticket is a standalone slice that rounds out the studio's online presence.

milestones.title: M10 - Polish, SEO & Security
milestones.description: Cross-cutting improvements: global toast system, reusable UI components, SEO meta tags, image optimization on upload, security headers, and rate limiting. Each ticket applies retroactively to all earlier features.

milestones.title: M11 - Email Notifications
milestones.description: Transactional emails: booking inquiry confirmation, status updates (confirmed, session complete, gallery ready, cancellation), and welcome email. Each ticket is a standalone mailable the learner can trigger and preview in Mailtrap.

milestones.title: M12 - Testing & Deployment
milestones.description: Automated tests covering auth, packages, booking flow, and authorization, plus the production deploy script. Each ticket is a test suite the learner runs and sees green, or a deployment artifact ready to use.

tickets.title: Remove starter kit boilerplate & set project identity
tickets.description: The Laravel starter kit ships with example pages, placeholder content, and demo components. Clean it up and brand it as HappyMom Photography.
tasks: Remove default welcome/dashboard page placeholder content (keep routes, clear component bodies)
Remove example/demo components that won't be reused
Clean up `AuthenticatedLayout` — strip demo nav links, keep the shell
Clean up `GuestLayout` — strip demo content, keep the shell
Update `app.blade.php` <title> to 'HappyMom Photography'
Update `.env` → `APP_NAME="HappyMom Photography"`
Review `resources/js/Pages/` — remove pages that won't be reused as-is
Keep all auth pages (Login, Register, ForgotPassword, ResetPassword) intact
acceptance_criteria: App loads with a clean, empty dashboard and homepage
No placeholder text or demo content remains
Auth pages still work (login, register, logout, forgot password)
APP_NAME shows 'HappyMom Photography' in browser tab
`npm run dev` and `php artisan serve` run without errors
learning_goals: Understanding Laravel starter kit structure
Identifying what to keep vs. remove when starting a new project
First contact with Inertia page components

tickets.title: Switch from SQLite to MySQL
tickets.description: The starter kit defaults to SQLite. Switch to MySQL for production readiness. Create the database, update config, and verify all existing migrations still work.
tasks: Create MySQL database `happymom_photography`
Update `.env`: `DB_CONNECTION=mysql`, `DB_DATABASE=happymom_photography`, set DB_HOST, DB_PORT, DB_USERNAME, DB_PASSWORD
Run `php artisan migrate` — all starter kit migrations must pass
Delete the SQLite file if present
Verify connection with `php artisan db:show`
acceptance_criteria: `.env` points to MySQL
All existing migrations (users, sessions, password_reset_tokens, etc.) run on MySQL without errors
`php artisan db:show` confirms MySQL connection and lists tables
SQLite file removed from project
learning_goals: Laravel database configuration
Understanding migrations as the source of truth for schema
Switching database drivers without touching migration files

tickets.title: Brand the app: Tailwind theme, fonts & colors
tickets.description: Customize the Tailwind config for HappyMom Photography's brand identity — soft, warm, and emotive. This is the visual foundation everything else builds on.
tasks: Define brand color palette in `tailwind.config.js`: blush rose as primary, warm cream as background, soft gold as accent, deep charcoal as text
Configure typography: serif font for headings (e.g., Cormorant Garamond or Playfair Display), sans-serif for body (e.g., Lato or Inter)
Import Google Fonts in `app.blade.php` or via CSS `@import`
Update `app.css` with base styles (body font, default text color, smooth scrolling, link hover transitions)
Test by adding a sample heading + paragraph to the dashboard to verify fonts and colors render
acceptance_criteria: Brand colors available as Tailwind classes (e.g., `bg-brand-blush`, `text-brand-gold`)
Serif heading font and sans-serif body font render correctly in the browser
Existing auth pages inherit the new theme automatically
No visual regressions on starter kit pages
learning_goals: Tailwind CSS configuration and design tokens
Google Fonts integration in Laravel/Vite projects
Building a cohesive brand palette as a shared foundation

tickets.title: Set up route structure & admin route file
tickets.description: Organize routes into three clear groups: public studio pages, client account, and admin panel. Add the admin route group alongside the starter kit's existing route files.
tasks: Create `routes/admin.php`
Register it in `bootstrap/app.php` with `prefix('admin')` and middleware `['auth']` (admin middleware added in M1)
Add a placeholder admin dashboard route: `GET /admin` → returns a simple Inertia page
Verify `web.php` serves public routes and `auth.php` is untouched
Run `php artisan route:list` to confirm route grouping
acceptance_criteria: `routes/admin.php` exists and is loaded with `/admin` prefix
`php artisan route:list` shows admin routes grouped under `/admin`
Placeholder `/admin` page renders for authenticated users
Public routes accessible without auth
Auth routes untouched and still working
learning_goals: Laravel routing: route groups, prefixes, and multiple route files
Middleware basics and where to apply it
Using `php artisan route:list` to debug route registration

tickets.title: Create AdminLayout with sidebar and render admin dashboard
tickets.description: Build the AdminLayout component and wire it to the admin dashboard route. After this ticket, navigating to /admin shows a branded admin shell with a sidebar — the first visible result of the admin section.
tasks: Create `resources/js/Layouts/AdminLayout.jsx` with sidebar + top bar + main content area
Sidebar placeholder links: Dashboard, Bookings, Packages, Categories, Gallery, Albums, Add-ons, Customers, Messages
Top bar: admin user name (from Inertia shared props via `usePage`) + logout button
Make sidebar responsive (collapsible hamburger menu on mobile)
Create `resources/js/Pages/Admin/Dashboard.jsx` — simple page with 'Admin Dashboard' heading wrapped in AdminLayout
Wire the `/admin` route to this page via a controller or closure
acceptance_criteria: Navigating to `/admin` (authenticated) shows the AdminLayout with sidebar
All sidebar links are visible (even if destination pages don't exist yet)
Top bar shows the admin user's name and a working logout button
Responsive: sidebar collapses on mobile via hamburger
AdminLayout is visually distinct from AuthenticatedLayout
learning_goals: React layout components and composition
Inertia shared props with `usePage()`
Responsive sidebar pattern with Tailwind
Separating admin and public layout concerns

tickets.title: Extend GuestLayout into a studio website shell with navbar & footer
tickets.description: The starter kit's GuestLayout is a minimal centered card for auth forms. Extend it into a full photography studio site layout with navbar and footer, while keeping auth pages looking correct.
tasks: Add a public navbar: studio logo/wordmark ('HappyMom Photography'), nav links (Home, Portfolio, Packages, About, Contact), auth links (Login / Book Now or user dropdown when logged in)
Make navbar sticky on scroll and responsive (hamburger on mobile)
Add a footer: studio tagline, quick links (Portfolio, Packages, About, Contact), social media icons (Instagram, Facebook), copyright with dynamic year
Auth pages should still render as a centered card within this layout — use a `variant` prop or a separate AuthLayout to differentiate
Create or update the homepage route (`/`) to render a simple placeholder page using this layout
acceptance_criteria: GuestLayout renders navbar + footer on all public pages
Auth pages (login, register) still display as centered form cards
Navbar is responsive with hamburger on mobile
Homepage placeholder renders at `/` with full layout
Footer shows dynamic current year
learning_goals: Extending existing layout components without breaking them
React props for layout variants
Responsive sticky navigation with Tailwind
Thinking ahead about layout variants (public vs auth)

tickets.title: Set up Git repo, branching strategy & push to GitHub
tickets.description: Initialize version control and establish the workflow the learner will use for every future ticket.
tasks: Initialize Git repo (if not already done by starter kit)
Verify `.gitignore` covers `vendor/`, `node_modules/`, `.env`, `storage/*.key`
Create GitHub repo `happymom-photography` with a README describing the project
Push initial commit (cleaned-up starter kit)
Create `develop` branch from `main`
Set up branch protection on `main` (require PR before merging)
Document branching convention in README: `main`, `develop`, `feature/T-XXX-short-description`
acceptance_criteria: GitHub repo exists with the clean starter kit pushed
`.gitignore` prevents committing sensitive or generated files
Branch protection enabled on `main`
README documents the branching convention
`develop` branch exists
learning_goals: Git workflow and branch protection rules
GitHub repository setup
Professional branching strategy for solo and team projects

tickets.title: Add roles to users: migration, model update, admin seeder — verify in browser
tickets.description: Extend the existing user system with roles. One ticket, full stack, working result: write the migration, update the model, seed an admin user, log in as admin, and see the admin dashboard.
tasks: Create a NEW migration (never modify the starter kit's original): add `phone` (nullable string), `role` (enum: client/admin, default client), `is_active` (boolean, default true) to `users` table
Update `User` model: add fields to `$fillable`, add `$casts` for `is_active` (boolean)
Add helper methods to User: `isAdmin()`, `isClient()`, `scopeActive()`
Create `database/seeders/AdminUserSeeder.php`: create admin user (email: `admin@happymom-photography.com`, password: `password`, role: `admin`) using `firstOrCreate`
Register seeder in `DatabaseSeeder.php`
Run `php artisan migrate && php artisan db:seed`
Log in as admin in the browser — should reach the admin dashboard
acceptance_criteria: Migration runs on top of existing users table without errors
`$user->isAdmin()` returns true for admin role
`$user->isClient()` returns true for client role
`User::active()->get()` filters out inactive users
Admin user seeded and can log in
Seeder is idempotent (safe to run multiple times with no duplicates)
Admin sees the `/admin` dashboard after login
learning_goals: Laravel migrations to alter existing tables without touching originals
Eloquent model customization: fillable, casts, scopes, helpers
Database seeders and idempotency with firstOrCreate
Enum columns in MySQL

tickets.title: Admin middleware & role-based login redirect — full protection working
tickets.description: Build the admin gate and make login smarter. After this ticket: clients can't access /admin (403), admins land on /admin after login, clients land on the homepage. Testable immediately in the browser.
tasks: Create `app/Http/Middleware/EnsureUserIsAdmin.php` — check `$user->isAdmin()`, abort 403 if not
Register middleware alias `admin` in `bootstrap/app.php`
Apply `admin` middleware to all routes in `routes/admin.php`
Modify post-login redirect: if admin → `/admin`, if client → `/`
Test: register a new client account → try to visit `/admin` → get 403
Test: log in as admin → land on `/admin` dashboard
acceptance_criteria: Non-admin users get 403 on all `/admin/*` routes
Admin users pass through the middleware without issue
Admin login → redirects to `/admin`
Client login → redirects to `/` (homepage)
Unauthenticated users → redirected to login page
Intended URL still works: accessing /admin while logged out → login → /admin
learning_goals: Laravel middleware creation and registration
Route middleware groups and aliases
Authentication redirects based on user role
HTTP 403 responses

tickets.title: Restyle auth pages & add phone to registration — branded auth flow
tickets.description: Restyle all four auth pages to match the HappyMom Photography brand and add a phone field to registration. After this ticket: the auth flow looks professional and captures phone numbers for booking contact.
tasks: Restyle Login page: brand colors, studio logo/wordmark above the form, styled inputs and buttons, soft background
Restyle Register page: same brand treatment, add optional phone input field
Restyle Forgot Password and Reset Password pages: consistent brand styling
Backend: add `phone` to registration validation rules (nullable, string) and `User::create()` call
Test: register with phone → saved to DB. Register without phone → still works
Test: all four auth pages are responsive on mobile
acceptance_criteria: All four auth pages visually match the HappyMom Photography brand
Studio logo/wordmark displays on all auth pages
Phone field is optional on registration and saved to DB when provided
No functional regressions (all auth flows work as before)
Forms are responsive and accessible
learning_goals: Styling Inertia/React pages with Tailwind CSS
Modifying starter kit auth controllers safely
Form validation rules in Laravel (nullable fields)
Extending existing registration forms

tickets.title: Move profile page to /account/profile & add phone field
tickets.description: Customize the starter kit's profile page: move it to the client panel URL, add the phone field, and restyle. After this ticket: clients can edit their profile including phone at /account/profile.
tasks: Update route: change `/profile` to `/account/profile`
Add phone field to the profile edit form (frontend)
Update the profile controller to validate and save phone
Restyle the page to match the studio brand
Remove or redirect the old `/profile` route
Test: edit name, email, phone → all saved. Delete account → still works
acceptance_criteria: Profile page accessible at `/account/profile`
Phone field editable and saved to DB
Brand styling applied consistently
Delete account functionality still works
Old `/profile` route no longer accessible (redirects or 404)
learning_goals: Modifying starter kit routes without breaking auth flows
Extending existing controllers
Route naming conventions for user panels

tickets.title: Session categories: migration + model + admin create form + admin list page
tickets.description: First real feature vertical slice! Build the session_categories table, model, admin create form, and admin list page in ONE ticket. After this ticket: the admin can create a session category (e.g. 'Newborn', 'Maternity') via a form and see it in a table.
tasks: Create `session_categories` migration: id, name, slug (unique), description (nullable), cover_image (nullable), is_active (boolean default true), sort_order (integer default 0), timestamps
Create `SessionCategory` model: fillable, slug auto-generation from name (boot method), `scopeActive()`, `packages()` hasMany placeholder
Configure storage: run `php artisan storage:link`, create `storage/app/public/categories/` directory
Create `AdminCategoryController` with `index`, `create`, and `store` methods
Create React page `Admin/Categories/Index.jsx` — table: name, slug, active badge, sort order, actions. Use AdminLayout
Create React page `Admin/Categories/Create.jsx` — form: name, description, cover image upload, is_active toggle, sort order. Use Inertia `useForm`
Add routes in `routes/admin.php`: GET `/admin/categories`, GET `/admin/categories/create`, POST `/admin/categories`
Wire flash message for success feedback after creation
Run migration, create a category via the form, see it in the list
acceptance_criteria: Migration runs, `session_categories` table exists in MySQL
Slug auto-generates from name (e.g., 'Newborn Sessions' → 'newborn-sessions')
Admin can fill the create form and submit
Validation: name required, slug unique
Cover image uploads to `storage/app/public/categories/`
After creation, admin redirected to list page with success flash
List page shows all categories in a table with active/inactive badges
Non-admin users get 403
learning_goals: Full vertical slice: migration → model → controller → React pages
Eloquent model with auto-slug boot methods
Inertia useForm hook for form state and submission
File upload handling in Laravel
Flash messages with Inertia

tickets.title: Session categories: edit, update & delete
tickets.description: Complete the session category CRUD. After this ticket: admin can edit any category's details, replace its cover image, and delete categories that have no packages attached.
tasks: Add `edit` and `update` methods to `AdminCategoryController`
Add `destroy` method — only allow deletion if the category has no packages (check relationship count)
Create React page `Admin/Categories/Edit.jsx` — pre-populated form, show current cover image with option to replace it
Add routes: GET `/admin/categories/{category}/edit`, PUT `/admin/categories/{category}`, DELETE `/admin/categories/{category}`
Add edit/delete action buttons to the categories list page
Add a confirmation modal before delete
Test: edit a category → changes saved. Delete empty category → removed. Delete category with packages → error message shown
acceptance_criteria: Edit form loads with all existing data pre-populated
Cover image can be replaced or kept unchanged
Validation errors display inline
Category with packages cannot be deleted (error message shown)
Empty category deletes successfully after confirmation
Flash messages for success and error states
learning_goals: Edit forms with Inertia (pre-populated data)
PUT requests via Inertia
Conditional deletion logic using relationship count

tickets.description: Seed realistic session categories and display them on the public studio site. After this ticket: an 'Our Sessions' section appears on the homepage and /portfolio/{slug} shows the category landing page.
tasks: Create `SessionCategorySeeder`: seed 5 categories — Newborn, Maternity, Sitter & Cake Smash, Family Portraits, Motherhood — with descriptions and photography-appropriate slugs

Create `CategoryController@show` — find by slug, pass to Inertia page, abort 404 if inactive/missing
Create React page `Portfolio/Category.jsx` — shows category name, description, cover image header, and a placeholder for the gallery grid (empty for now). Use GuestLayout
Add route: GET `/portfolio/{slug}`
Update the homepage placeholder: add an 'Our Sessions' section showing the 5 category cards (cover image, name, short description, link to category page)
Create `HomeController@index` — pass active categories to the homepage
Test: visit homepage → see category cards → click one → land on the category page
acceptance_criteria: 5 session categories seeded with realistic photography descriptions
Homepage 'Our Sessions' section shows category cards
Clicking a card navigates to `/portfolio/{slug}`
Category page shows name, description, and cover image header

tickets.title: Packages: migration + model + admin create form (all fields) + admin list page
tickets.description: The core sellable unit. Create the packages table, model, and admin interface to create and list packages. After this ticket: admin can create a package (e.g. 'Mini Newborn Session — €250') with all details and see it in a paginated list.
tasks: Create `packages` migration: id, session_category_id (FK), name, slug (unique), tagline (nullable string), description (text), price (decimal 10,2), duration_minutes (integer), photo_count (integer), outfit_changes (integer default 0), inclusions (JSON — array of strings), is_active (boolean default true), is_featured (boolean default false), sort_order (integer default 0), timestamps, soft_deletes

Create `Package` model: fillable, casts (price as decimal, inclusions as array, booleans), `sessionCategory()` belongsTo, scopes: `active()`, `featured()`, `formattedPrice` accessor returning '€XX', SoftDeletes trait
Create `AdminPackageController` with `index`, `create`, and `store` methods
Create React page `Admin/Packages/Index.jsx` — table: name, category, price, duration, featured badge, status badge, actions. Pagination (15/page). Category filter dropdown

Create React page `Admin/Packages/Create.jsx` — form: name, category dropdown, tagline, description, price, duration (minutes), photo count, outfit changes, inclusions (dynamic add/remove list), is_active toggle, is_featured toggle, sort order
Add routes: GET `/admin/packages`, GET `/admin/packages/create`, POST `/admin/packages`
Run migration, create a package via the form, see it in the admin list
acceptance_criteria: Packages table created with correct FK to session_categories and soft_deletes
Package model has working scopes, casts, and relationships
Admin can fill the full create form including dynamic inclusions list
Validation: name, category, price, duration, photo_count required

Slug auto-generates from name
Package list paginated with category filter working
Non-admin users get 403
learning_goals: Complex migration with JSON column type
Eloquent model casts for arrays and decimals
Dynamic list inputs in React (add/remove inclusions)

Create React page `Admin/Packages/Edit.jsx` — pre-populated form with all fields; inclusions list pre-loaded and editable

tasks: Create `AdminDashboardController@index` — query: total packages, total categories, total clients, pending inquiries (0 placeholder for now), upcoming confirmed sessions (0 placeholder for now)

tickets.title: Portfolio gallery: migration + model + admin image upload + admin list
tickets.description: Build the portfolio gallery management. After this ticket: the admin can upload portfolio images, tag them by session category, and see them in a grid. This is the studio's primary trust-building asset.
tasks: Create `gallery_images` migration: id, session_category_id (nullable FK), title (nullable), image_path, alt_text (nullable), caption (nullable), is_featured (boolean default false), sort_order (integer default 0), timestamps

Create `AdminGalleryController` with `index`, `create`, and `store` methods. Support multi-file upload (upload multiple images at once)
Create React page `Admin/Gallery/Index.jsx` — image thumbnail grid with category badge, featured star toggle, delete button. Category filter at top. AdminLayout
Create React page `Admin/Gallery/Create.jsx` — multi-file input (drag-and-drop preferred), category dropdown, alt text, caption, featured toggle
Add routes: GET `/admin/gallery`, GET `/admin/gallery/create`, POST `/admin/gallery`
Run migration, upload a batch of images, verify they appear in the admin grid
acceptance_criteria: Migration runs, `gallery_images` table created
Admin can upload multiple images in one submission
Images stored in `storage/app/public/gallery/`
Admin grid shows thumbnails with category badges
Category filter narrows the grid correctly
Non-admin users get 403
learning_goals: Multi-file upload in Laravel
Nullable foreign keys and optional relationships
Image grid admin UI with filter
Drag-and-drop file input in React

tickets.description: Build the public portfolio page. After this ticket: visitors can browse the studio's full portfolio in a beautiful grid and filter by session type.
tasks: Create `GalleryController@index` — return active gallery images with eager-loaded categories, optionally filtered by category slug from query param
Create React page `Portfolio/Index.jsx` — responsive image grid, category filter tabs at top (All, Newborn, Maternity, etc.), image hover overlay with caption. Use GuestLayout

Test: visit `/portfolio` → see all images. Click 'Newborn' → only newborn images. Click 'All' → all images return
acceptance_criteria: Portfolio page shows all gallery images in a responsive grid

tickets.title: Package detail page: gallery preview, inclusions, and booking CTA
tickets.description: Build the single package detail page. After this ticket: clicking a package leads to a full detail page with inclusions checklist, sample gallery images from the same category, and a 'Book This Session' CTA.
tasks: Create `PackageController@show` — find by slug, eager-load sessionCategory with up to 6 featured gallery images. Abort 404 if inactive/missing

Create React page `Packages/Show.jsx`: hero with package name + tagline, price prominently displayed, duration and photo count at a glance, full description, inclusions as a checklist, sample gallery grid (6 images from same category), 'Book This Session' CTA button (placeholder for now), 3 related packages from same category. Use GuestLayout
Add SEO meta tags via Inertia `<Head>`: dynamic title and description
Add route: GET `/packages/{slug}`
Test: click a package → detail page loads with all info. Gallery shows images from same category. Invalid slug → 404
acceptance_criteria: Package detail page renders all fields with inclusions as a checklist
Duration formatted as hours (e.g., '2 hours')
Price formatted with € symbol
Sample gallery shows up to 6 images from the same session category
3 related packages shown from same category
SEO title and description set dynamically
404 for inactive or non-existent packages
learning_goals: Route model binding with slug columns
Eager loading nested relationships (category → gallery images)
Inclusions checklist UI
SEO with Inertia Head component

tickets.title: Booking inquiry: tables + models + service + form + confirmation (full vertical slice)
tickets.description: The primary transaction of the app. Build the entire booking inquiry flow in ONE ticket. After this ticket: a logged-in client selects a package, fills in session details, submits the booking, and lands on a confirmation page. The admin dashboard pending count increments.
tasks: Create `bookings` migration: id, user_id (FK), package_id (FK), booking_number (unique), status (enum: inquiry/confirmed/session_completed/editing/gallery_ready/cancelled, default inquiry), preferred_date_1 (date), preferred_date_2 (date nullable), confirmed_date (date nullable), session_location (string nullable), baby_due_date (date nullable), baby_birth_date (date nullable), special_requests (text nullable), subtotal (decimal 10,2), total (decimal 10,2), admin_notes (text nullable), timestamps

Create `Booking` model: fillable, casts (dates, decimals), `user()` belongsTo, `package()` belongsTo, status helpers (`isInquiry()`, `isConfirmed()`, etc.), static `generateBookingNumber()` (e.g., HMP-20260001), `formattedTotal` accessor
Create `BookingService@createBooking(user, package, data)`: DB transaction → generate booking number → create booking with total copied from package price → return booking
Create `BookingController`: `create` (show form, auth required, pass package via `?package={slug}` query param), `store` (validate and call service)

Create React page `Bookings/Create.jsx`: package summary card at top (name, price, inclusions at a glance), form fields: preferred date 1 (date picker, required, future dates only), preferred date 2 (optional), baby due date (if applicable), baby birth date (if applicable), session location preference, special requests (textarea). 'Submit Inquiry' button
Create React page `Bookings/Confirmation.jsx`: thank-you message, booking number, package name, preferred dates, 'View My Bookings' and 'Return to Homepage' links
Add routes: GET `/bookings/create`, POST `/bookings`, GET `/bookings/{booking}/confirmation`
Wire the 'Book This Session' CTA on the package detail page to `/bookings/create?package={slug}`
Redirect unauthenticated users to login, then back to the booking form
Update `AdminDashboardController` so the pending inquiries count is now live
acceptance_criteria: Bookings table created with all fields and correct MySQL types
Booking form pre-loads the selected package details from the query param
Validation: preferred_date_1 required and must be a future date
Submitting creates a booking in DB with status 'inquiry' and unique HMP-XXXXXXXX number
Total copied from package price at time of booking
Confirmation page shows booking number and summary
Unauthenticated users redirected to login then back to form
DB transaction: booking either fully created or not at all
Admin dashboard pending count now reflects real data
learning_goals: Full vertical slice across all layers in one ticket
Service pattern for isolating business logic
Date picker with future-date validation
DB transactions for data integrity
Booking/order number generation pattern
Redirecting guests to login and back (intended URL)

Update `BookingService@createBooking`: create booking_addons records, recalculate total (package price + selected add-ons)
Update `Bookings/Confirmation.jsx`: list selected add-ons and the updated total
Test: book with add-ons → total includes them. Book without → still works

tasks: Create a `useSeo({ title, description, image })` custom hook that renders Inertia `<Head>` with title, meta description, og:title, og:description, og:image, og:type

Package detail pages: og:image uses a featured gallery image from the same session category
Test: inspect `<head>` source for each public page → correct unique tags present
acceptance_criteria: Every public page has a unique `<title>`
Meta description set per page
Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:image) set on key pages
Package pages use dynamic data for meta tags
useSeo hook is reusable with a simple API
learning_goals: SEO fundamentals for Inertia/React SPAs
Inertia Head component
Custom React hooks
Open Graph protocol for social sharing previews

tickets.title: Image optimization on upload
tickets.description: Automatically optimize images when uploaded. After this ticket: all gallery and cover images are resized, compressed, and have thumbnails generated — reducing storage and improving page load speed.
tasks: Install Intervention Image library (`intervention/image`)
Create an `ImageService`: resize to max 1920px width, compress to 80% quality, generate a 400px thumbnail
Store both optimized image and thumbnail with naming convention (`{hash}.jpg`, `{hash}_thumb.jpg`)
Integrate into gallery image upload (`AdminGalleryController`) and category cover image upload (`AdminCategoryController`)
Add `thumbnailUrl` accessor to GalleryImage model alongside existing `url`
Test: upload a large 5MB image → stored file is significantly smaller. Thumbnail exists. Both URLs accessible in browser
acceptance_criteria: Gallery images auto-resized to max 1920px width and compressed
400px thumbnails generated for every upload
Original file not retained — optimized version replaces it
Thumbnails used in admin gallery grid and public portfolio grid
Noticeable file size reduction compared to original upload
learning_goals: Image processing in PHP with Intervention Image
Service pattern for cross-cutting upload logic
Thumbnail generation strategy
Storage optimization for media-heavy applications

tickets.title: Security headers & rate limiting on sensitive endpoints
tickets.description: Harden the app with security headers and protect sensitive endpoints from abuse. After this ticket: the app has production-grade security headers and rate limiting on auth and booking routes.
tasks: Verify CSRF protection is active for all Inertia POST/PUT/DELETE requests (starter kit should handle this — confirm with a manual test)
Add security headers via middleware: X-Frame-Options (DENY), X-Content-Type-Options (nosniff), Referrer-Policy (strict-origin-when-cross-origin)
Add rate limiting: registration (3/min), contact form (5/min), booking inquiry (10/min). Verify starter kit's login throttling exists
Review all models: verify no model uses `$guarded = []`
Create user-friendly 429 response: show a clear message rather than a raw error
Test: inspect response headers in DevTools → security headers present. Submit booking 11 times rapidly → rate limited with friendly message
acceptance_criteria: CSRF validation active on all state-changing requests
Security headers present in all responses
Registration, contact form, and booking inquiry rate-limited
429 responses show user-friendly message
No model uses unguarded mass assignment
learning_goals: Web security headers and why they matter
CSRF protection in Inertia apps
Laravel throttle middleware
Mass assignment protection in Eloquent

tickets.title: Email setup & booking inquiry confirmation email
tickets.description: Configure email and send the first transactional email. After this ticket: submitting a booking inquiry triggers a branded confirmation email to the client.
tasks: Configure mail driver in `.env` (Mailtrap for dev, or log driver as fallback)
Customize default Laravel mail template: HappyMom Photography logo, brand colors (blush/warm palette), footer with studio contact info
Create `BookingInquiryReceivedMail` mailable: booking number, package name, preferred dates, special requests summary, 'View Your Booking' link, 'What happens next?' section
Trigger the email in `BookingService::createBooking()` after successful booking creation
Test: submit a booking → email delivered to Mailtrap. Email contains booking number, correct package name, branding
acceptance_criteria: Mail driver configured and receiving emails in Mailtrap
Mail template branded with HappyMom Photography identity
Booking confirmation email sent after inquiry submission
Email contains: booking number, package name, preferred dates, 'View Booking' link
Link points to `/account/bookings/{booking}`
learning_goals: Laravel mail configuration and Mailtrap setup
Mailable classes and their structure
Blade email templates with custom branding
Triggering emails from service classes

tickets.title: Booking status update emails
tickets.description: Send emails to clients when the admin updates a booking's status. After this ticket: clients are automatically notified at each key stage of their session journey.
tasks: Create `BookingStatusUpdatedMail` mailable with contextual messaging per status: confirmed ('Your session is confirmed for {date}!'), session_completed ('Your photos are being edited — we can't wait to share them!'), editing ('Editing is in progress'), gallery_ready ('Your gallery is ready to view!')
Create a separate `BookingCancelledMail` with an apologetic tone and studio contact info
Trigger status emails from `AdminBookingController@updateStatus` after the status change
Test: change status to 'confirmed' in admin → client receives email with confirmed date. Change to 'gallery_ready' → client receives celebratory email
acceptance_criteria: Status update email sent on each status change from admin
Messaging is contextual and appropriate per status
Confirmed email includes the confirmed session date
Gallery ready email has a celebratory tone
Cancellation email uses a separate mailable with apologetic messaging
All emails use the branded HappyMom Photography template

Client communication design in booking applications

tickets.title: Welcome email on registration
tickets.description: Send a warm welcome email when a new client registers. After this ticket: every new account receives a branded welcome message with a booking CTA.
tasks: Create `WelcomeEmail` mailable: warm welcome message with the client's first name, studio intro paragraph, 'Browse Our Packages' CTA linking to /packages, photographer's name in the closing
Implement `ShouldQueue` on the mailable for async delivery so registration is not slowed down
Trigger after successful registration (hook into the registered event or add to the RegisteredUserController)
Test: register a new account → welcome email queued and delivered asynchronously. Email contains CTA link and personal tone
acceptance_criteria: Welcome email sent after every new registration
Email is queued (async — does not block the registration redirect)
Contains 'Browse Our Packages' CTA link to /packages
Warm, personal tone appropriate for a photography studio
Branded with HappyMom Photography template

Async vs. synchronous email sending and why it matters
Registration event hooks in Laravel

tickets.title: Test environment setup & first passing test
tickets.description: Set up the testing environment and write the first passing tests. After this ticket: `php artisan test` runs green.

Verify `RefreshDatabase` trait resets state between tests

Run `php artisan test` → all green

At least 2 passing tests

Writing the first green test

tickets.title: Feature tests: authentication & role-based access
tickets.description: Write tests covering the full auth flow and role-based access controls. After this ticket: the auth system and admin gate are fully tested.
tasks: Test registration: valid data → user created and logged in. Invalid (duplicate email, missing name) → validation errors
Test login: valid credentials → authenticated. Wrong password → error. Inactive user → blocked
Test logout: session destroyed, redirected to /
Test admin middleware: client → 403 on /admin. Admin → 200. Unauthenticated → redirect to login
Test role-based redirect: admin login → /admin. Client login → /
Minimum 10 test cases total
acceptance_criteria: All auth flows tested with correct assertions
Admin middleware tested for all three actor types (admin, client, guest)
Inactive user login blocked and tested
Role-based redirects tested
All tests pass
learning_goals: Feature testing in Laravel
Testing authentication flows end-to-end
Testing middleware authorization
Assertion methods: assertAuthenticated, assertRedirect, assertStatus

tickets.title: Feature tests: packages & categories admin CRUD
tickets.description: Write tests for admin package and category management. After this ticket: the core content management features are fully tested including authorization.
tasks: Test category creation: valid data → category in DB with auto-generated slug. Invalid (missing name) → validation error
Test category edit and delete (empty category deletes, category with packages rejects deletion)
Test package creation: valid data → package in DB with inclusions JSON. Invalid data → validation errors
Test package soft delete: soft-deleted → gone from list, still in DB with deleted_at timestamp
Test authorization: client accessing admin routes → 403. Unauthenticated → redirect to login
Test public packages page: returns only active packages. Soft-deleted packages not shown
acceptance_criteria: Category and package CRUD operations fully tested with DB assertions
Slug auto-generation tested
Authorization tested for all admin routes
Soft delete behavior tested
Public pages only show active, non-deleted content
All tests pass
learning_goals: Testing CRUD with database assertions
Testing authorization scenarios (403)
Testing slug generation
Testing JSON columns and soft deletes

tickets.title: Feature tests: booking inquiry flow
tickets.description: Write tests covering the complete booking inquiry pipeline. After this ticket: the booking flow is fully tested from form submission to admin status update.
tasks: Test booking creation: authenticated client + valid data → booking in DB with status 'inquiry' and correct HMP-XXXXXXXX number format
Test booking creation: unauthenticated → redirect to login
Test booking creation: invalid data (missing preferred_date_1, past date) → validation errors
Test add-ons: booking with add-ons → booking_addons records created, total calculated correctly
Test authorization: client can view own booking (200). Client cannot view another client's booking (403)
Test admin status update: valid transition creates history record. Invalid transition is rejected
Minimum 8 test cases
acceptance_criteria: Booking creation tested with DB state assertions (booking number format, status, total)
Authentication requirement tested
Future-date validation tested
Add-on price calculation tested
Authorization on booking access tested
Status transition logic tested for valid and invalid cases
Status history creation tested
All tests pass

Testing service classes in feature tests
Testing status machine transitions
Testing audit trail (history record) creation

tickets.title: Production environment config & deploy script
tickets.description: Prepare the app for production deployment. After this ticket: a deploy script automates the full deployment process and the app is production-ready.
tasks: Create `.env.production.example` with all required vars: APP_ENV=production, APP_DEBUG=false, DB credentials, mail config, APP_URL=https://happymom-photography.com, FILESYSTEM_DISK=public, QUEUE_CONNECTION=database
Document server requirements in README: PHP 8.3+, MySQL 8+, required PHP extensions (GD or Imagick for image processing), Node.js for asset build, storage symlink

Create `deploy.sh` script: `npm run build`, `php artisan migrate --force`, `php artisan storage:link`, `php artisan config:cache`, `php artisan route:cache`, `php artisan view:cache`, `php artisan optimize`
Create production seeder: creates only the admin account, no sample or demo data
Verify `npm run build` produces optimized assets with cache-busted filenames
Document queue worker setup in README (required for async welcome email from T-047)
Document rollback procedure
acceptance_criteria: `.env.production.example` committed with all necessary variables documented
README has a complete server requirements section
`deploy.sh` automates full deployment in one command
Production seeder creates only the admin account
`npm run build` produces minified, versioned assets
Queue worker requirement documented for async mail
learning_goals: Production deployment configuration for Laravel
Laravel optimization commands and what each one does
Environment-specific seeders
Build pipeline automation
Queue worker requirements for async features
